title: Validate an IPv4 Address

problem.statement: Fill in the missing loop body so is_valid_ipv4 returns True only for a real IPv4 address: exactly 4 numeric octets, each 0-255, with no leading zeros like '01'.
problem.code_context:
def is_valid_ipv4(ip: str) -> bool:
    parts = ip.split('.')
    if len(parts) != 4:
        return False
    for p in parts:
        ____
    return True

# is_valid_ipv4("192.168.1.1")  -> True
# is_valid_ipv4("256.1.1.1")    -> False (out of range)
# is_valid_ipv4("01.2.3.4")     -> False (leading zero)
# is_valid_ipv4("10.0.0")       -> False (only 3 parts, caught above)
problem.use_case: Input validation like this guards every script that accepts an IP from a CSV, an API payload, or a CLI argument — a single unvalidated bad octet can silently break a downstream Netmiko connection or Ansible inventory build.

hints: Each part must be all digits — str.isdigit() catches non-numeric input.
Leading zeros ('01') should be rejected even though int('01') == 1 — check len(p) > 1 and p[0] == '0'.
if not p.isdigit() or (len(p) > 1 and p[0] == '0') or not 0 <= int(p) <= 255: return False

solution.explanation: The full check is: if not p.isdigit() or (len(p) > 1 and p[0] == '0') or not 0 <= int(p) <= 255: return False — reject non-digit octets, reject leading zeros, and range-check 0-255.
